name: Mount Tallac Trail
description: Challenging climb to the summit of Mount Tallac at 9,735 feet with stunning panoramic views of Emerald Bay, Cascade Lake, Fallen Leaf Lake, and the entire South Shore. One of Lake Tahoe's most iconic hikes.
parking_info: Large paved parking area at trailhead on Mount Tallac Road. Arrive early on weekends—fills by 8am in summer.

name: Eagle Lake Trail
description: Moderate hike through Desolation Wilderness leading to the picturesque Eagle Lake. Trail offers beautiful alpine scenery and is accessible from Emerald Bay.
permits_required: Desolation Wilderness day-use permit required in summer (free, self-issue at trailhead)
parking_info: Bayview Campground trailhead parking on Hwy 89. Day-use fee applies. Overflow on shoulder of Hwy 89.

name: Cascade Falls Trail
description: Popular short hike to a spectacular 200-foot waterfall with views of Cascade Lake and Lake Tahoe. Trail starts from Bayview Campground near Emerald Bay.
parking_info: Bayview Campground parking area off Hwy 89. Day-use fee. Fills quickly on summer weekends.

name: Rubicon Trail
description: Scenic shoreline trail along Lake Tahoe's southwest corner, contours between D.L. Bliss and Emerald Bay State Parks with continuous panoramic lake views.
parking_info: D.L. Bliss State Park entrance parking or Emerald Bay day-use lot. Shuttle recommended for point-to-point.

name: Hidden Falls Trail
description: Beautiful hike through Sierra Foothills leading to hidden waterfalls. Network of trails up to 11 miles with mix of easy and moderate terrain. Dog-friendly on leash.
parking_info: Large free parking area at trailhead off Hwy 49 S in Auburn. Open sunrise to sunset.

name: American River Parkway Trail
description: World-renowned 32-mile paved multi-use trail from Discovery Park in Sacramento to Beal's Point in Folsom. Follows the American River with mile markers, water fountains and restrooms.
parking_info: Multiple access points along the trail. Discovery Park and Beal's Point have large lots. Street parking available at many trail access points.

name: Cosumnes River Preserve Trail
description: Easy loop trail through wetlands, riparian forest, and grasslands. Home to over 250 species of birds. Includes paved boardwalk to observation platform.
parking_info: Free paved parking lot at the preserve visitor center on Franklin Blvd in Galt.

name: Bassi Falls Trail
description: Moderate hike in El Dorado National Forest to a stunning 109-foot waterfall. Best visited in spring and early summer when water flow is strongest.
parking_info: Small dirt pullout at trailhead on Bassi Road off Ice House Road. Very limited space—arrive early.

name: Stevens Trail
description: Popular 8-mile round trip trail descending through forests along the American River. Features canyon views, wildflowers in spring, and river access for swimming.
parking_info: Trailhead parking on Canyon Way in Colfax. Free gravel lot with room for about 20 cars.

name: Lake Natoma Loop Trail
description: Scenic 11.5-mile loop around Lake Natoma providing serene water views. Mostly flat, paved trail perfect for hiking, biking, and running.
parking_info: Parking at Negro Bar State Park or Nimbus Flat. Day-use fee applies at state park lots.

name: Granite Lake and Maggie's Peaks
description: Most popular and challenging trail in South Lake Tahoe. Steep climb to peaks at around 8,500 feet elevation with spectacular alpine views.
permits_required: Desolation Wilderness permit required for overnight; day use self-issue
parking_info: Bayview Campground trailhead off Hwy 89. Parking limited—arrive before 8am on summer weekends.

name: Tahoe Meadows Interpretive Loop
description: Gentle family-friendly loop through beautiful subalpine meadows. Wildflower displays in summer, educational interpretive signs along the trail.
parking_info: Large free parking area off Hwy 431 (Mount Rose Hwy) near the summit. Open when road is clear of snow.

name: Fallen Leaf Lake Trail
description: Easy 2-mile hike along the scenic shores of Fallen Leaf Lake. Flat, well-maintained trail with serene lake views through pine forest.
parking_info: Small gravel lot at the end of Fallen Leaf Lake Road. Street parking available along the road.

name: Auburn State Recreation Area - Confluence Trail
description: Popular trail in Auburn SRA leading to the confluence of the North and Middle Forks of the American River. Moderate difficulty with river views.
parking_info: Parking at Auburn SRA trailhead off Cool-Foresthill Rd. Day-use fee. Multiple lots along Hwy 49.

name: Cronan Ranch Loop
description: Scenic loop trail through oak woodlands and along the South Fork American River. Part of Cronan Ranch Regional Trails Park with historic ranch views.
parking_info: Free parking at the Cronan Ranch trailhead on Salmon Falls Road off Hwy 49.

name: Miwok Trail - Lake Natoma
description: Peaceful singletrack trail on the south side of Lake Natoma through oak woodland and native grassland. Great wildlife viewing and quieter than the main parkway trail.
parking_info: Parking at Sailor Bar County Park off Illinois Avenue in Orangevale. Free lot.

name: Granite Chief Trail
description: Challenging high-alpine trail from Squaw Valley to the Granite Chief Wilderness summit with panoramic views of Tahoe and Desolation Wilderness.
permits_required: Wilderness permit required for Granite Chief Wilderness (free self-issue)
parking_info: Trailhead at Squaw Valley Ski Resort base area. Parking in resort lots—day-use fee on weekends.

name: Shirley Canyon Trail
description: Family-friendly canyon trail in Squaw Valley with cascading waterfalls and granite boulders. A local favorite for summer afternoon hikes.
parking_info: Squaw Valley base area parking off Olympic Valley Rd. Large lot—day use fee on weekends.

name: Euchre Bar Trail
description: Remote and rugged hike dropping steeply to the North Fork American River. Features swimming holes and dramatic canyon scenery. Best for experienced hikers.
parking_info: Small dirt pullout on Hwy 49 near the Euchre Bar bridge. Very limited—4-6 cars only.

name: Loch Leven Lakes Trail
description: Gorgeous Sierra Nevada trail passing through forest and granite to three serene alpine lakes. Popular with families and backpackers.
parking_info: Trailhead at Loch Leven parking area off I-80 at the Big Bend exit near Soda Springs.
